evidence_quotes.quote: Loot boxes should show the probability of each item?
higher amounts have a better exchange rate?

paragraphs.text: It is good that the commission is looking at dark patterns, but the current paper lacks specifics. Personal interest to me is the gaming angle. Will the proposal include: - Loot boxes should show the probability of each item? - Requirement to have parental controls on in-game microtransaction purchases when the developer has reached some income target? - Requirement to have spending limit controls on microtransactions in-game for gambling addicts? - Bans on selling in-game currency in set amounts, where higher amounts have a better exchange rate? Also, since the citizen initiative to end publisher practices of destroying art they sold to consumers has reached signature goals, maybe it would be good to handle video games as a specific issue.
